response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong full-stack and software development skills with a focus on testability and user experience, but his experience lacks direct alignment with the core requirements of the Principal Performance Architect role, such as SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, or deep hardware-software co-design. His background in cloud infrastructure and performance optimization is limited, though his work with low-latency systems and embedded projects shows some transferable skills.
strengths: Extensive experience with low-latency and high-performance systems (e.g., DLang at Dunnhumby, real-time note-taking apps).
Strong full-stack development background with exposure to cloud infrastructure (AWS, Kubernetes) and open-source contributions.
Proven ability to lead projects and mentor others, as evidenced by workshops, talks, and open-source tools (e.g., Fluent-Asserts, Trial).
concerns: No explicit experience with SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, or GPU architecture, which are core to the role.
Limited focus on performance analysis for deep-learning workloads or hardware-software co-design trade-offs.
Most recent roles emphasize full-stack development and AI-agent infrastructure, diverging from the hardware-centric requirements.
